STRAIGHT LINE WINDS PUSHED HOME OFF PILINGS AND REMOVED ALL ROOFING MATERIAL FROM ADJACENT HOME. STORM WITNESSED BY EM DIRECTOR.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE HAS SURVEYED AREA AND HAS DETERMINED THAT DAMAGE WAS PRODUCED BY A TORNADO. INITIAL TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED APPROX. 4 MILES SOUTHWEST OF CARLINVILLE AT 1130 AM...DESTROYING A LUMBER SHED. TWO GRAIN BINS ALSO SUSTAINED SERIOUS DAMAGE...ONE OF WHICH WAS BLOWN OFF FOUNDATION AND THE SECOND HAD ITS ANCHOR BOLTS SHEARED OFF. TORNADO THEN MOVED NORTHEAST DAMAGING POWER POLES...AND LIFTED NEAR HIGHWAY 4 ABOUT 1.5 MILES NORTH OF CARLINVILLE AT 1135 AM AFTER DESTROYING A SMALL SHED AND OUTBUILDING. DAMAGED ESTIMATED AS EF1.
